name: cpig-booleans-02-difference-sphere-sphere
description: Phase D4 boolean audit (B0) — SIGN CONVENTION CHECK. Difference of two CPig tape spheres. The CPig convention (research dive §1.2: field_binary_impl 'difference' is max(fa->tree, -(fb->tree))) keeps the FIRST-picked operand and subtracts the second. So _SelAll picks A then B (whichever order Rhino delivers); _CPigDifference keeps A, subtracts B. With A=sphere(0,0,0;r=10) and B=sphere(15,0,0;r=10), expect: a SINGLE sphere centered near origin with a CRESCENT BITE taken out of its +x side (B subtracted). If we see a sphere at +x with a bite from its -x side, the operand order was reversed — that's the surface to watch (research dive B3: prompt doesn't clarify which is minuend).

setup.vlmDescription: A 3D ray-marched signed-distance surface showing one sphere with a curved bite taken out of one side (a crescent moon shape, or a sphere minus a sphere). NOT a single intact sphere, NOT a peanut, NOT two separate spheres.

checkpoints.name: cpig-booleans-difference-sphere-sphere
checkpoints.description: Difference(sphere_A, sphere_B) — one sphere with a curved bite taken out of one side. Operator should verify WHICH side has the bite to confirm sign convention.
